title: trichonema azureum
side_effects: Trichonema azureum can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal problems. Ingesting large quantities of the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while topical application can cause redness, itching, and burns. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can also cause respiratory issues, such as coughing and shortness of breath. In severe cases, improper use of Trichonema azureum can lead to anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction that requires immediate medical attention.
